Title: Welsh nationalists poised for historic election breakthrough
Subhead: For the first time in its history, the pro-independence Welsh party Plaid Cymru could top the Welsh Parliament in Thursday's election. The surge reflects not just disillusionment with rivals but a deeper frustration at years of being sidelined by London.

Title: Hantavirus: Many unknowns surround an 'unprecedented and worrying' outbreak
Israel PM says targeted a top Hezbollah commander in Beirut strike
Moscow's Bauman University, the clandestine school training Russian hackers
Subhead: According to 2,000 documents obtained by a consortium of international media outlets, including Le Monde, the prestigious Bauman Moscow State Technical University has been discreetly training Russian military intelligence officers, particularly hackers. These lists of students and professors, along with course plans, provide an unprecedented glimpse into the 'militarization of education' as part of the hybrid war waged by Vladimir Putin's regime.

Title: Gaza police become targets for Israeli army
Subhead: Police forces under Hamas's authority are regularly targeted by the Israeli army. Israel describes these as preventive strikes, which target officers who also serve in the Islamist movement's armed wing. Civil society actors, on the other hand, accuse Israel of seeking to plunge the territory into chaos.

Title: PSG knock out Bayern to lock Champions League final with Arsenal
Subhead: With a 1-1 draw, Paris Saint-Germain sealed a 6-5 aggregate victory at Bayern Munich on Wednesday.

Title: French presidential election: Proposals abound, but who will champion them?
Subhead: While the abundance of proposals is welcome, their impact should not be overstated, writes Le Monde columnist Françoise Fressoz. The Socialists, for example, have worked on a policy platform but can't agree on how to choose a candidate.

Louvre President Christophe Leribault: 'I am not here to undo the work of my predecessors'
Subhead: In an interview, the successor to Laurence des Cars as head of the museum explains how he intends to make his mark with humility, courage and ambition.

Title: Europeans rethink collective defense amid Trump's threats against NATO
Subhead: Would the US intervene in the event of an attack on Europe? European leaders, who met Monday in Yerevan alongside Canada, are growing increasingly doubtful after Trump announced the withdrawal of 5,000 American troops from Germany.

Title: Hundreds of flight cancellations leave passengers frustrated and stranded
Subhead: The war in the Middle East has caused the price of jet fuel to double in just a few weeks. Many airlines, especially low-cost carriers, have canceled flights with low occupancy or low profitability. Passenger rights are protected, but within certain limits.
